revision.text:
[[ja:浅井神社 (一宮市)]] {{Expand Japanese|浅井神社 (一宮市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya) | native_name = 浅井神社 (一宮市) | image = Azai-jinja in Ichinomiya ac (2).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya) | coordinates = {{coord|35.333537|136.824128}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|lt=Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|pl|Mizuhanome|sv|Mizuhanome|zh|彌都波能賣神|qid=Q8191796|1=Mizuhanome}} }} {{nihongo|'''Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya)'''|浅井神社 (一宮市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 14 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039467|ltq=Q135039467|qid=Q135039467|1=Nomino Shrine (Q135039467)}} ** P155: {{ill|Harikumano Shrine|lt=Harikumano Shrine|qid=Q135039466|1=Harikumano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039469|ltq=Q135039469|qid=Q135039469|1=Mokuhino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11556984|ltq=Q11556984|qid=Q11556984|1=Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Ichinomiya, Aichi|lt=Ichinomiya|ar|إيتشينوميا (آيتشي)|arz|ايتشينوميا|azb|ایچینومیا، آیچی|az|İçinomiya|bg|Ичиномия|ceb|Ichinomiya (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Итиномия|ckb|ئیچینۆمیا، ئایچی|cs|Ičinomija (Aiči)|dag|Ichinomiya|de|Ichinomiya (Aichi)|en|Ichinomiya, Aichi|envoyage|Ichinomiya|eo|Iĉinomija|es|Ichinomiya (Aichi)|eu|Ichinomiya (Aichi)|fa|ایچینومیا، آیچی|favoyage|ایچینومیا، آیچی|fi|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichinomiya|ga|Ichinomiya|gl|Ichinomiya|hy|Իչինոմիա|id|Ichinomiya, (Aichi)|no|Ichinomiya|pl|Ichinomiya|pt|Owari-ichinomiya|ro|Ichinomiya, Aichi|ru|Итиномия|sh|Ichinomija|simple|Ichinomiya, Aichi|sr|Ичиномија|sv|Ichinomiya|tg|Ичиномия|th|อิจิโนมิยะ (จังหวัดไอจิ)|tl|Ichinomiya, Aichi|tr|Ichinomiya|tt|Итиномия|uk|Ітіномія (Айті)|ur|اچینومیا، ایچی|vi|Ichinomiya, Aichi|war|Ichinomiya, Aichi|wuu|一宫市|zh_classical|一宮市|zh_min_nan|Itinomiya-chhī|zh_yue|一宮|zh|一宮市|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Nakashima district|lt=Nakashima district|ja|中島郡|zh|中島郡|qid=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}} * {{ill|Q128846124|ltq=Q128846124|qid=Q128846124|1=Q128846124}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.333537|136.824128}} == image (P18) == * Azai-jinja in Ichinomiya ac (2).jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q8191796|ltq=Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|1=Mizuhanome}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181356 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == different from (P1889) == * {{ill|Asawino Shrine|lt=Asawino Shrine|qid=Q135039477|1=Asawino Shrine (Q135039477)}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9180005009738 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市浅井町東浅井北屋舗浦312', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町宮後', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県稲沢市浅井町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:浅井神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11556984}} == Japanese content == {{Confusion|link1=Azai Shrine (Inazawa City)|1=Azai Shrine|x1=the one in Inazawa City, Aichi Prefecture}} {{Citations needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | Name = Azai Shrine | Image = [[File:Azai-jinja in Ichinomiya ac (2).jpg|250px]]
Worship hall | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Azai-chō Higashiazai|Q126916764|qid=Q126916764|1=Azai-chō Higashiazai}} Kitayashikiura 312 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} / former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | Founded = {{ill|lt=563|Q33782|qid=Q33782|1=563}} (24th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}}) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Azai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Azai-jinja in Ichinomiya ac (1).jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii]] '''Azai Shrine''' (あざいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Azai-chō (Aichi Prefecture)|Q11556970|qid=Q11556970|lt=Azai-chō|1=Azai}}. === Overview === Its former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking system|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was village shrine. It is also written as '''淺井神社'''. It is the “Azai Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|qid=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}} in {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki shrine register|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. According to the Engishiki Jinmyōchō, there are two Azai Shrines in Nakajima District, Owari Province. This article describes the Azai Shrine in Ichinomiya City. For the Azai Shrine in Inazawa City, see {{ill|lt=Azai Shrine (Inazawa City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Azai Shrine}}. Although this area belongs to {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} of Owari Province, Azai Shrine is an Engishiki shrine of Nakajima District, Owari Province. Similarly, there is a theory that “{{ill|lt=Ishito Shrine|Q11585184|qid=Q11585184|1=Iwato Shrine}},” an Engishiki shrine of Nakajima District, Owari Province, corresponds to {{ill|lt=Ishito Shrine#Ishito Shrine (Azai-chō, Ichinomiya City)|Q11585184|qid=Q11585184|lt=Ishito Shrine (Azai-chō, Ichinomiya City)|1=Iwato Shrine}}. There is also a theory that the Engishiki shrine “Kosai Shrine” of Nakajima District corresponds to {{ill|lt=Kosai Shrine|Q11459557|qid=Q11459557|1=Ozeki Shrine}}. As this region is an area frequently affected by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}, it is said that the district boundaries since the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} are not clearly defined; therefore, this area may also have belonged to Nakajima District. The area where Azai Shrine stands is near the borders of Haguri District, Nakajima District, and {{ill|lt=Niwa District|Q1347719|qid=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}}. === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=563|Q33782|qid=Q33782|1=563}} (24th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}}). The shrine buildings stand atop a kofun (Azai Shrine Kofun), from which items such as gold rings and iron arrowheads have been excavated. This area contains many small kofun collectively known as the Azai Kofun Group, and {{ill|lt=Kosai Shrine|Q11459557|qid=Q11459557|1=Ozeki Shrine}}, also an Engishiki shrine, likewise has its shrine buildings on a kofun. The shrine has deep connections with water, and it is said that one of the Three Famous Springs of Owari, “Azai no Onkoi,” once existed here. The name Onkoi (温故井) remains at Onkoi Pond in {{ill|lt=Azaiyama Park|Q11556931|qid=Q11556931|1=Azaiyama Park}} south of Azai Shrine (a former tributary of the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}}). During the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Hachiryūsha, and the enshrined deity was believed to be a {{ill|lt=dragon god|Q1206058|qid=Q1206058|1=Dragon god}}. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|qid=Q7720|1=1869}} (2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}), it was renamed back to Azai Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} (5th year of Meiji) it became a village shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Kami|1=Mizuhanome}} ; Jointly enshrined * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji}} === Cultural properties === * Artifacts excavated from the Azai Kofun Group — Designated cultural property of Ichinomiya City === Access === * About an 8-minute walk from the “Azai” bus stop on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}. * From {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|qid=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bisai Line|Q901005|qid=Q901005|lt=Bisai Line|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|qid=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} or {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|qid=Q11235139|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|qid=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} (Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal, platform 3), take a bus bound for “Miyata Hongō.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Azai Shrine (Ichinomiya)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
